text:
There was a young fox who loved painting. He spent endless nights staring at canvases, blending blues and reds onto his masterpieces. One evening, while hanging his latest piece outdoors, a wise owl perched beside him.

"You love painting," remarked the owl. "You could do anything. Why do you think?"

The fox shrugged proudly. "I prefer painting."

The owl tilted his head thoughtfully. "Perhaps."

One morning, while the fox was painting the sunrise, an unusual visitor appeared. It was an old owl friend who seldom flew this way anymore.

"Listen," said the old owl. "You've mastered your art. Why do you keep painting?"

"I couldn't help it," replied the fox. "Just because I can."

The old owl flew away silently, thinking perhaps about duty and desires. Days passed. One evening, the fox hung his painting once more.

"This time," he whispered softly, "I want to share."

He showed his old friend.
